sourceTitle: How to build an effective go-to-market (GTM) plan

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a Go‑To‑Market (GTM) plan?

options.text: To outline the financial budget for a product.
options.explanation: A GTM plan focuses on launch strategy and steps, not specifically on budgeting.

options.text: To describe the strategy and steps for launching a new product or feature.
options.explanation: Correct – a GTM plan defines how a product will be introduced to the market.

options.text: To create a detailed technical specification for developers.
options.explanation: Technical specs are separate from the market‑focused launch plan.

options.text: To generate a list of potential investors for funding.
options.explanation: Investor lists are not the main focus of a GTM plan.

questions.question: When setting up a GTM plan in Notion, which database property is NOT mentioned as part of the default Projects database?

options.text: Launch date
options.explanation: Launch date is listed as one of the default properties.

options.text: DRI (Directly Responsible Individual)
options.explanation: DRI is explicitly mentioned as a default property.

options.text: Priority level
options.explanation: Priority level is included in the default set.

options.text: Customer acquisition cost
options.explanation: Customer acquisition cost is not listed among the default properties.

questions.question: Why should you add a table of contents at the top of your GTM plan page?

options.text: It automatically generates a project timeline.
options.explanation: A table of contents provides navigation, not a timeline.

options.text: It allows readers to quickly jump to each major section.
options.explanation: Correct – the TOC improves navigation across sections like About, Execution, and Launch Day.

options.text: It creates a visual background color for the page.
options.explanation: Background colors are set via block styling, not through a TOC.

options.text: It links the GTM plan to external YouTube videos.
options.explanation: Linking videos is done via embed blocks, not by a table of contents.

questions.question: Which Notion feature helps you keep track of all meeting notes related to your GTM plan?

options.text: Relation property
options.explanation: The Relation property lets you link other pages, such as meeting notes, to the GTM plan.

options.text: Formula property
options.explanation: Formulas calculate values; they don’t create links between pages.

options.text: Rollup property
options.explanation: Rollups aggregate data from related items but require a Relation first.

options.text: Toggle block
options.explanation: Toggle blocks hide content; they don’t establish cross‑page connections.

questions.question: How can you make your GTM plan page more visually engaging in Notion?

options.text: Add background colors and create columns by dragging blocks side‑by‑side.
options.explanation: Correct – customizing background colors and arranging columns enhances visual appeal.

options.text: Increase the page’s font size globally via the settings menu.
options.explanation: Notion does not provide a global font‑size setting for a single page.

options.text: Insert a spreadsheet from Google Sheets to replace the Notion database.
options.explanation: Embedding a spreadsheet is possible but does not directly affect Notion’s visual styling.

options.text: Enable dark mode for only the GTM plan page.
options.explanation: Dark mode applies to the entire workspace, not a single page.

questions.question: What advantage does using a template from Notion’s template gallery provide when building a GTM plan?

options.text: It automatically syncs the GTM plan with external CRM systems.
options.explanation: Templates do not include built‑in CRM integration; they are pre‑designed page structures.

options.text: It gives you a pre‑structured layout with common sections and properties, saving setup time.
options.explanation: Correct – templates offer ready‑made sections (e.g., About, Execution) and relevant properties, accelerating creation.

options.text: It locks the page so team members cannot edit any content.
options.explanation: Templates are editable; they do not enforce read‑only permissions.

options.text: It provides AI‑generated market analysis data within the plan.
options.explanation: AI‑generated data is not a built‑in feature of Notion templates.

questions.question: Which combination of Notion features best supports assigning actionable tasks to team members within a GTM plan?

options.text: Checklists, @‑mentions, and dynamic dates.
options.explanation: Checklists create tasks, @‑mentions notify responsible people, and dynamic dates keep timelines current.

options.text: Headers, background colors, and table of contents.
options.explanation: These improve organization and aesthetics but don’t directly assign tasks.

options.text: Relation property, rollup, and formula.
options.explanation: These are for linking and calculating data, not for task assignment.

options.text: Embedded YouTube video, image block, and toggle.
options.explanation: Multimedia blocks enhance content richness but don’t manage task delegation.
